nodes.title: Handle empty root up front
nodes.why: This avoids crashes and gives a clear result when traversal starts with no node.
nodes.alternative: Let the function fail when root is missing.
nodes.tradeoff: One extra check, but safer behavior and cleaner caller experience.

nodes.title: Test current node before going deeper
nodes.why: If the current node matches, we can return immediately and skip all children.
nodes.alternative: Always search children first, then test current node.
nodes.tradeoff: Current-first keeps DFS simple and usually faster for high-level matches.

nodes.title: Traverse children with DFS
nodes.why: Recursion lets each child subtree use the same logic without duplicate code.
nodes.tradeoff: DFS recursion is concise, but very deep trees can increase call stack depth.

nodes.title: Stop at the first found match
nodes.why: The problem asks for the first match, so there is no reason to keep searching after success.
nodes.alternative: Collect all matches and return a list.
nodes.tradeoff: Early return is faster, but it only gives one match.

nodes.title: Return null when nothing matches
nodes.why: A clear no-result value makes calling code simple to handle.
nodes.alternative: Throw an error when not found.
nodes.tradeoff: `null` is easier for flow control, but callers must still remember to check it.
